title: Divide by 3
description: Divide by 3.  Quotients are less than or equal to 10.
topic_path: Module 1: Properties of multiplication and division and solving problems with units of 2–5 and 10
Topic D: Division using units of 2 and 3

hints:
If we split $\blueD{12}$ circles into $\greenD3$ equal rows, how many circles are in each row?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{12} \div \greenD{3} = {4}$

If we split $\blueD{27}$ circles into $\greenD3$ equal rows, how many circles are in each row?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{27} \div \greenD{3} ={9}$

If we split $\blueD{30}$ circles into $\greenD3$ equal rows, how many circles are in each row?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{30} \div \greenD{3} = {10}$

If we split $\blueD{18}$ circles into $\greenD3$ equal rows, how many circles are in each row?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{18} \div \greenD{3} = {6}$

If we split $\blueD{9}$ circles into $\greenD3$ equal rows, how many circles are in each row?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{9} \div \greenD{3} = {3}$

If we split $\blueD{21}$ circles into $\greenD3$ equal rows, how many circles are in each row?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{21} \div \greenD{3} = {7}$

If we split $\blueD{15}$ circles into $\greenD3$ equal rows, how many circles are in each row?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{15} \div \greenD{3} ={5}$

If we split $\blueD{3}$ circles into $\greenD3$ equal rows, how many circles are in each row?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{3} \div \greenD{3} = {1}$

If we split $\blueD{24}$ circles into $\greenD3$ equal rows, how many circles are in each row?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{24} \div \greenD{3} = {8}$

If we split $\blueD{6}$ circles into $\greenD3$ equal rows, how many circles are in each row?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{6} \div \greenD{3} = {2}$
